name: Sourdough Upkeep
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the sourdough culture from the refrigerator.
2. Divide the culture into two parts and place each part in its own quart-sized wide mouth jar or plastic container.
3. Add ½ cup warm water (95°F to 100°F) and ½ cup white bread flour to each of the cultures.
4. Place the lids on the jars, but do not tighten them securely.
5. Place the jars in a proofing box set to 85°F. Leave the jars in the proofing box until foamy bubbles appear. This may take several hours or up to several days depending on how long the culture was dormant.
Once the culture is ready and foaming, it can be used without commercial yeast for baking.
